title: aeschynanthus sanguineus
intro: Aeschynanthus sanguineus, also known as the 'Mist Maiden' or 'Christmas Bells', is a type of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Asia, specifically in the Philippines and Malaysia. It is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, and its unique bell-shaped flowers are highly fragrant and attract hummingbirds. The medicinal properties of Aeschynanthus sanguineus include its antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Aeschynanthus sanguineus include infusions and decoctions, which can be consumed as teas or used topically to harness its medicinal benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
